title: Reducing household paper: switching bills, managing documents, and opting out of junk mail
subtitle: The practical steps to paperless billing, document scanning, and mail suppression for a UK household
excerpt: A typical UK household receives 70-100 items of direct mail and printed bills per year. Switching to e-billing, registering with the Mailing Preference Service, and scanning incoming documents reduces paper waste to a few kilograms per year and simplifies record-keeping. Digital document storage with a consistent naming convention makes retrieval faster than a filing cabinet.
sourceNotes: Mailing Preference Service (MPS) opt-out registration at mpsonline.org.uk. Royal Mail door-to-door opt-out service for unaddressed mail. ICO (Information Commissioner's Office) data retention guidance for personal records. Which? guide to paperless banking and utility billing. WRAP paper recycling statistics for UK households.

content.text: Paper arrives unsolicited from many sources: utility bills, bank statements, council tax, catalogues, charity appeals, and unaddressed leaflets. Each has a different suppression route. Switching all of them to digital takes about two hours and reduces ongoing paper to a trickle.

Addressed direct mail (companies you haven't bought from recently): register with the Mailing Preference Service at mpsonline.org.uk. Free registration removes your name from most commercial mailing lists within 2-4 months.

Unaddressed leaflets and catalogues: Royal Mail offers a door-to-door opt-out form at royalmail.com. This stops unaddressed items delivered by Royal Mail. Some items are delivered by private leaflet distribution companies and are not affected.
Charity appeals: contact each charity individually to ask them to suppress mailing. Under GDPR, charities must comply if you withdraw consent for postal marketing.
Utilities and broadband: log into your account online and find 'paperless billing' or 'go paperless' in account settings. Most UK providers offer a small monthly discount for paperless billing.
Bank statements: switch via the bank's app or online portal. Keep digital statements for 6 years for tax and financial dispute purposes.
Council tax, housing benefit, electoral registration: contact your local council directly. Many UK councils have moved to e-correspondence but require you to opt in via an account portal.

A smartphone with document scanning (iOS Files, Google Drive, or a dedicated app) makes a searchable PDF from a flat document. Use a consistent naming convention such as Year-Month-Day_Description.pdf. This files documents chronologically and makes retrieval fast. Store in a cloud folder with offline backup. Keep physical originals only for passports, birth certificates, property deeds, and signed legal documents. Most households have around 10-15 of these.
